2604.23129.title: MindTrellis: Co-Creating Knowledge Structures with AI through Interactive Visual Exploration
2604.23129.paper_summary: Knowledge workers face increasing challenges in synthesizing information from multiple documents into structured conceptual understanding. This process is inherently iterative: users explore content, identify relationships between concepts, and continuously reorganize their mental models. However, current approaches offer limited support. LLM-based systems let users query information but not shape how knowledge is organized; manual tools like mind maps support structure creation but lack intelligent assistance. This leaves an open opportunity: supporting collaborative construction where users and AI jointly develop an evolving knowledge representation. We present MindTrellis, an interactive visual system where users and AI collaboratively build a dynamic knowledge graph. Users can query the graph to retrieve document-grounded information, and contribute by introducing new concepts, modifying relationships, and reorganizing the hierarchy to reflect their developing understanding. In a user study where 12 participants created slide decks, MindTrellis outperformed retrieval-only baselines in knowledge organization and cognitive load, as measured by expert ratings of content coverage and structural quality.
2604.23129.paper_summary_zh: 知識工作者在將多個文件中的資訊綜合成結構化的概念理解時面臨越來越多的挑戰。<lb/>這個過程本質上是迭代的：用戶探索內容，識別概念之間的關係，並不斷重組他們的心理模型。<lb/>然而，目前的做法提供的支持有限。<lb/>基於LLM的系統允許用戶查詢資訊，但無法塑造知識的組織方式；像思維導圖這樣的手動工具支持結構創建，但缺乏智能輔助。<lb/>這留下了一個開放的機會：支持協作構建，讓用戶和AI共同發展一個不斷演變的知識表徵。<lb/>我們提出了MindTrellis，一個互動式視覺系統，讓用戶和AI共同構建一個動態知識圖譜。<lb/>用戶可以查詢該圖譜以檢索基於文件的信息，並通過引入新概念、修改關係和重組層級來反映他們不斷發展的理解。<lb/>在一項用戶研究中，12名參與者創建了幻燈片，MindTrellis在知識組織和認知負荷方面超越了僅限檢索的基準，這是通過專家對內容覆蓋和結構質量的評價來衡量的。
2604.23129.author: Xiang Li et.al.
2604.23129.authors: Xiang Li, Cara Li, Emily Kuang, Can Liu, Jian Zhao
